parts: Name this figure who is the title character of that Offenbach opera and is the first title character of a Gluck opera about him and Euridice.
Gluck also composed two operas about this character “en Aulide” and “en Tauride.” The latter work ends with Diana demanding her statue be rebuilt and the title character be allowed to return home.
This Italian composer was inspired by Gluck’s Don Juan to write his Symphony in D major and also wrote a String Quintet in E major with a famous third movement.

bonuses.leadin: The oboe represents the crow of a cock, which scares away the central figures of this symphonic poem, which are represented by xylophones. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this symphonic poem that depicts dancing skeletons, and was parodied in the section “Fossils” in Carnival of the Animals.
Danse Macabre is a work by this French composer of Carnival of the Animals.
This section from the Carnival of the Animals parodies Offenbach’s Orpheus in the Underworld by slowing it down immensely to depict the title animals.
